steps.title: Solve the first equation for x
steps.content:
The first equation $5x = 15$ is already isolated. Divide both sides by 5:
$$x = \frac{15}{5} = 3$$

steps.title: Substitute x into the second equation and solve for y
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 3$ into the second equation $-4x + y = -2$:
$$-4(3) + y = -2$$
$$-12 + y = -2$$
Add 12 to both sides:
$$y = 10$$

steps.title: Calculate the sum x + y
steps.content:
Add the two values:
$$x + y = 3 + 10 = 13$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
